title: T-127 / W0-DATA ‚Äî Walking Skeleton real-data wiring (dashboard summary + module landings, org-scoped Supabase via withOrgContext)
prompt:
# T-127 / W0-DATA ‚Äî Walking Skeleton real-data wiring

PRD: Walking Skeleton (Wave 0) Definition of Done ‚Äî `/kira:skeleton` DoD #4.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Replace the i18n-only placeholder content on the dashboard and module landing pages with REAL, org-scoped data read from Supabase through `withOrgContext` (RLS `org_id = app.current_org_id()`), so the menu-driven product shows live DB data ‚Äî the Wave 0 Definition of Done. Honest-maximal scope: every module whose domain table exists in the schema today is wired to a live count; modules without a domain table yet render a clearly-marked honest stub (live data lands in their own wave).

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(modules)/_actions/skeleton-data.ts` [create] ‚Äî server-only module (NOT 'use server'; called directly from Server Components). Exports `getModuleCount(table)` and `getOrgSummary()`. Each wraps `withOrgContext(async ({ client }) => client.query('select count(*)::int ...'))` so reads run as `app_user` inside `app.set_org_context`. Fixed allowlist of schema-qualified tables (`public.bom_item|work_order|quality_event|shipment|lot|users`) ‚Äî no caller input interpolated into SQL. Failures are logged + surfaced as `{ ok:false }` / null (graceful, never silent).
2. `_components/module-data-panel.tsx` + `_components/module-stub-notice.tsx` [create] ‚Äî presentational Server Components; all copy passed pre-localized.
3. Real-data pages [modify] ‚Äî dashboard (6-metric org summary), technical‚Üíbom_item, production‚Üíwork_order, quality‚Üíquality_event, shipping‚Üíshipment, warehouse‚Üílot. Preserve existing `data-testid=module-landing-<id>` + heading; add `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'`.
4. Honest-stub pages [modify] ‚Äî planning, scheduler, finance, reporting, maintenance, oee, multi-site, npd render `<ModuleStubNotice>`.
5. i18n `Skeleton` namespace added to all four locales (`apps/web/i18n/{en,pl,uk,ro}.json`).
6. Tests ‚Äî `_actions/__tests__/skeleton-data.test.ts` (vitest, mocks withOrgContext; allowlist mapping, zero-row, error degrade, summary). `apps/web/e2e/walking-skeleton.spec.ts` (executable DoD: login ‚Üí shell ‚Üí full menu click-through ‚Üí data renders; strict numeric assertion gated behind WALKING_SKELETON_REQUIRE_DB against a real Supabase target).

## Acceptance criteria
1. Dashboard renders `data-testid=dashboard-org-summary` with 6 metric tiles fed by `getOrgSummary()` (org-scoped via withOrgContext/RLS).
2. Each of technical/production/quality/shipping/warehouse renders `data-testid=module-live-data` from its allowlisted public table; the 8 table-less modules render `data-testid=module-stub-notice`.
3. All reads are org-scoped through `withOrgContext` (no raw `current_setting`, no cross-org leakage); failures degrade to an honest 'unavailable' state, never a 500 or a mock.
4. Gates green: vitest unit + nav/contract suites, `tsc --noEmit`, `eslint`, `next build`.

## Out of scope
- Domain schemas/tables for modules that don't exist yet (each ships in its own wave).
- Replacing the working login/shell/nav (audited ‚úÖ, not rebuilt).

## Closeout evidence
changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations, git_status.

pipeline_inputs.description: Wire the dashboard summary + module landing pages to real org-scoped Supabase data via withOrgContext; honest stubs where domain tables don't exist yet. The DoD #4 'real data, not mocks' gate for the Walking Skeleton.
out_of_scope: Domain schemas/tables for modules that have none yet (built in their own wave).
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard renders data-testid=dashboard-org-summary with 6 metric tiles fed by getOrgSummary() (org-scoped via withOrgContext/RLS).
technical/production/quality/shipping/warehouse each render data-testid=module-live-data from their allowlisted public table; the 8 table-less modules render data-testid=module-stub-notice.
All reads are org-scoped through withOrgContext (no raw current_setting, no cross-org leakage); failures degrade to an honest 'unavailable' state, never a 500 or a mock.
Gates green: vitest unit + nav/contract suites, tsc --noEmit, eslint, next build.
test_strategy: Unit: vitest mocks withOrgContext, asserts allowlist‚Üípublic.* mapping, zero-row=0, error‚Üí{ok:false}, summary all-6 and all-null-on-failure.

E2E: apps/web/e2e/walking-skeleton.spec.ts ‚Äî login‚Üíshell‚Üíclick every sidebar item‚Üíassert module landing + live-data/stub; strict numeric assertion behind WALKING_SKELETON_REQUIRE_DB against a real Supabase target.
risk_red_lines: Never interpolate caller input into SQL ‚Äî only the fixed schema-qualified allowlist.
All DB reads go through withOrgContext (RLS app.current_org_id()); never raw current_setting and never bypass org scoping.
Do not present mock/hardcoded data as real; a failed read must render an honest 'unavailable' state, not fabricated numbers.
Preserve module-landing-<id> data-testids and headings (route-contract + parity gates).
